{{Quote|Grab his ankles and get ready to pull him out.|Yun, giving orders to stormtroopers while trying to save Jaru|<ref name="DF: JK" />}}
'''Jaru''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] serving the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] during his search for the [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]] called the [[Valley of the Jedi]]. When the Valley was finally discovered in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], Jaru was among the stormtroopers assigned to excavate it. After a worldquake shook Ruusan, Jaru was trapped under a massive piece of ceiling but was rescued by [[Yun]], one of Jerec's [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]].

==Biography==
[[File:Usetheforceyun.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Yun uses the Force to save Jaru.]]
Jaru was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] during his search for the long-lost [[Valley of the Jedi]], a powerful [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]] that contained numerous trapped [[Force ghost/Legends|Jedi souls]].<ref name="DF: JK" /> In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].</ref> Jerec discovered the Valley on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] and dispatched a detachment of stormtroopers to begin excavation works there. Jaru was one of the dispatched [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], who were commanded by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Vig]].<ref name="DF: JK" />

Days after the excavation works began, a worldquake shook Ruusan. When it occurred, Jaru was standing near a cargo module inside one of the Valley's underground chambers. As a result of the worldquake, a massive piece of ceiling fell on Jaru, who survived only because he had been bending over at the moment. The cargo module was half-crushed but absorbed the initial impact of the cave-in and held the slab aloft. Still, Jaru was trapped beneath a boulder the size of a [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|hovercraft]],<ref name="DF: JK" /> which began to inch down, threatening to crush him.<ref name="DF: JK audio">[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]]</ref> An [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]], several troopers, and two construction [[Droid/Legends|droids]] tried but failed to lift the boulder off Jaru. Moreover, another set of tremors hit, causing large chunks of rock to fall and [[Death/Legends|kill]] one of the other troopers.<ref name="DF: JK" />

The rescue attempt was witnessed by [[Yun]], a member of Jerec's [[Seven Dark Jedi|cadre]] of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], who was overseeing the excavation works. Driven close to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] by the spirit of the late [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]], Yun decided to save Jaru. Using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], he [[Telekinesis/Legends|lifted]] the rock into the air, allowing the officer and the troopers to pull Jaru out. The troopers then loaded Jaru onto a makeshift stretcher and carried him to the surface.<ref name="DF: JK" />

==Personality and traits==
Jaru was well known among Jerec's troops for the size of his nose and the ability to spit farther than anyone else in his unit. He was also skilled with a [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launcher]].<ref name="DF: JK" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jaru first appeared in ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'', a novella written by [[William C. Dietz]]<ref name="DF: JK" /> that was published on [[October 14]], [[1998]].<ref name="Date">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/95-348/Star-Wars-Dark-Forces---Jedi-Knight-GSA-HC|text=Star Wars: Dark Forces - Jedi Knight GSA HC}}</ref> and later appeared in the [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|audio dramatization]] of the novel,<ref name="DF: JK audio" /> which was released in the same year.<ref name="HighBridge">{{HighBridge|url=starwardarfo5.html|text=''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight''}}</ref> The audio drama contradicts the novel in a number of ways, stating that it was a piece of wall that fell on Jaru<ref name="DF: JK audio" /> instead of a piece of ceiling.<ref name="DF: JK" /> Also, construction droids do not participate in the first rescue attempt, and are instead estimated to arrive in three minutes. Additionally, in the audio drama, the rescue team is commanded by Major Vig himself.<ref name="DF: JK audio" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' {{1st}}
